Exposure Analysis
Geopolitical (27%)
Hormuz closure persists (CF + GTT). S3 base case 55%. Kharg Island struck — oil infrastructure war expanding. Both sides reject ceasefire. Allied naval coalition forming.
Micro-Execution (57%)
VOW Maritime margin at ~68% new-contract mix → Q2 2026 result validates 18.8% central estimate. CF NOLA-ME spread convergence approaching S3 territory ($800/t).
Macro (33%)
No recession / LNG demand holds (GTT). Fertilizer demand intact — India Kharif season Apr-Jun (CF). No inflation re-acceleration compressing multiples.
Market Structure
VOW: zero sell-side coverage. CF: commodity spot-to-realized lag timing edge. GTT: FID-to-order conversion deferred catalyst.

25/25
Impact: 5/5 Discovery: 5/5 Deployed: 40%
Thesis: VOW SOTP implies +212% upside (NOK 7.54 vs 2.42) with imminent hard catalyst (Q1 results May 20, Q2 Aug 2026).

Hormuz Disruption
STABLE
20/25
Impact: 5/5 Discovery: 4/5 Deployed: 27%
Thesis: Hormuz closure (Feb 28) disrupts 33% seaborne fertilizer + 44% global sulphur. No strategic reserves exist. S3 base case (3-month, $800-1000/t urea) at 55%.
Scenarios: S1 5% (2wk, $530/t) · S2 15% (4wk, $610/t) · S3 55% (3mo, $850/t) · S4 25% (6mo, $1000/t)
Archive Trigger: Ceasefire + confirmed Hormuz reopening + mine clearance timeline
Latest Signal (Mar 18): Leadership decapitation (Larijani, Soleimani killed); Iran rejects ceasefire; India partial passage (22 vessels); Shah gas field suspended; Brent $103
